items.title: Airlines scramble to add Caribbean flights after airspace restrictions strand tens of thousands of travelers
items.description: U.S. airlines resumed flights to Puerto Rico and other Caribbean destinations early Sunday after aviation authorities lifted temporary airspace restrictions.

items.title: Golden Dome Project Begins With a Massive $151 Billion Contract
items.author: Rich Smith
items.description: President Trump's Golden Dome missile defense project has awarded a $151 billion contract through the SHIELD program to 2,100 contractors. The contract, administered by the U.S. Missile Defense Agency, represents the first phase of funding through 2035. Major defense contractors and satellite companies including Boeing, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, General Dynamics, RTX, Viasat, and Intuitive Machines have been selected as awardees, though specific contract values and task orders remain to be determined.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Confirmed as a SHIELD contract awardee, positioning the satellite company to benefit from the multi-billion dollar Golden Dome project funding over the next decade.
Major defense contractor selected as a SHIELD awardee, eligible to receive portions of the $151 billion contract for missile defense system development.
Major defense contractor selected as a SHIELD awardee, eligible to receive portions of the $151 billion contract for missile defense system development.
Large aerospace and defense specialist awarded SHIELD contract participation, providing access to significant government funding for Golden Dome project work.

Set to acquire Lanteris Space Systems, which was awarded SHIELD contract participation, providing exposure to Golden Dome project funding.

items.title: Airbus Said to Beat 2025 Annual Delivery Goal of 790 Aircraft
items.description: Airbus cut its previous 2025 guidance of about 820 handovers last month after a series of glitches involving its best-selling A320 model, including a massive recall for a software upgrade, followed by the discovery of fuselage panels on the same plane model that didn’t meet specifications and require extra inspections.  An Airbus spokesperson declined to comment on its annual deliveries, adding that audited orders and delivery figures will be published after markets close on Jan 12.  Aircraft deliveries at both Airbus and rival Boeing Co. are closely watched performance measures because they are the biggest source of cash and provide an indicator of supply network stability.
